records.ety: [OE. frost, forst, AS. forst, frost. fr. fresan to freeze; akin to D. varst, G., OHG., Icel., Dan., & Sw. frost. √18. See Freeze, v. i.]

defs: The act of freezing; -- applied chiefly to the congelation of water; congelation of fluids.
The state or temperature of the air which occasions congelation, or the freezing of water; severe cold or freezing weather.
quotes: The third bay comes a frost, a killing frost.

defs: Frozen dew; -- called also hoarfrost or white frost.
quotes: He scattereth the hoarfrost like ashes.

defs: Coldness or insensibility; severity or rigidity of character.
quotes: It was of those moments of intense feeling when the frost of the Scottish people melts like a snow wreath.
The brig and the ice round her are covered by a strange black obscurity: it is the frost smoke of arctic winters.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Frosted; p. pr. & vb. n. Frosting.]

defs: To injure by frost; to freeze, as plants.
To cover with hoarfrost; to produce a surface resembling frost upon, as upon cake, metals, or glass; as, glass may be frosted by exposure to hydrofluoric acid.
quotes: While with a hoary light she frosts the ground.

defs: To roughen or sharpen, as the nail heads or calks of horseshoes, so as to fit them for frosty weather.
